news.summary: Cuba’s government on Monday confirmed that it had recently met with U.S. officials on the island as tensions between the two sides remain high over the U.S. energy blockade of the Caribbean country. FRANCE 24's Ed Augustin reports from Havana.

news.headline: EU eyes Ukraine loan, Israeli settler sanctions, after Hungarian election
news.summary: Ouster of EU 'spoiler-in-chief' Viktor Orban unlocks key foreign policy decisions in the 27-member bloc.

news.headline: Apple says chief executive Tim Cook stepping down in September
news.summary:
<p>Apple on Monday announced that Chief Executive Officer (CEO) Tim Cook will step down in September, handing the job to company veteran John Ternus.</p>

news.summary: The global energy crisis has implications for both sides.

news.headline: U.S. and Iran Talks Appear on Track to Resume Despite Mixed Messages
news.summary: With the two-week cease-fire almost over, Vice President JD Vance was expected to head to Pakistan on Tuesday for the second round of negotiations.

news.headline: FBI Director Kash Patel sues Atlantic for ‘false’ reporting on drinking
news.summary: The Atlantic defends its reporting as Kash Patel denies allegations and files a $250m US defamation lawsuit.

news.headline: Elon Musk snubs Paris prosecutors' summons over X and Grok
news.summary: Elon Musk did not appear Monday for a voluntary interview with Paris prosecutors, who had issued the summons in February as part of an investigation into allegations that X's algorithm was used to interfere in French politics. news.summary: Governments and regulators are assessing potential implications and urging critical sectors to beef up their defences after a powerful new AI model sent shockwaves around the world. news.headline: Indian politicians are campaigning while holding fish. What is going on?
news.summary: In West Bengal polls, fish moves from plate to politics, symbolising identity and cultural pride.
